title: One Case, Whatever It Takes
tagline: A body at dawn, a nervous witness, and a captain who wants this closed by tonight.

wake_up.text: Your phone buzzes before your alarm does. "Body found near the river. Captain wants you on it personally." You're awake before you've decided to be.

choices.text: Make coffee before you do anything else
Grab your badge and go straight there

crime_scene.text: Tape's already up. Uniforms are keeping a small crowd back. The scene tells a story if you're patient enough to read it — and impatient enough not to lose the morning to it.

choices.text: Process every inch of the scene yourself before anyone else touches it
Get the fast read and let forensics handle the rest

witness_interview.text: A jogger found the scene and is still shaking. She saw something — you can tell — but she keeps stopping herself before she says it.

choices.text: Slow down, build trust, let her get there on her own time
Push — you don't have time for slow this morning

captain_pressure.text: The captain calls. "Press is already asking questions. I need something by end of day." You have a partial description and a theory, not a suspect.

choices.text: Promise a solid update by evening, no more
Push back — this needs the time it needs

evidence_lab.text: Forensics comes back faster than expected: a partial print and fiber evidence pointing at someone with a record two blocks away. It's thin, but it's something.

choices.text: Go talk to him

chase_scene.text: The moment he sees your badge, he bolts down the fire escape. You're not as young as you used to be, and neither is he.

choices.text: Chase him yourself, right now
Call it in and let backup box him in

interrogation_setup.text: He's in the box now, across the table, arms crossed. His story has holes in it. You can feel he's close to talking.

choices.text: Begin the interview

interrogation_room.text: "I know you were there," you say. He shifts, cracks just slightly — then asks for a lawyer. This is the moment that decides whether this case holds up in court, or falls apart in it.

choices.text: Keep pressing before the lawyer arrives
Offer a deal for cooperation
Stop and wait for his lawyer, by the book
You don't have enough — let him go for now

interrogation_outcome_press.text: He talks. It's not pretty, and the DA's office is going to have questions about how you got it — but you have a confession on record.

choices.text: Write it up

interrogation_outcome_deal.text: He gives you a name in exchange for a word to the DA. It's not the ending you wanted, but it's a lead that holds up.

choices.text: Write it up

interrogation_outcome_lawyer.text: The lawyer arrives, and with them, a clean, unimpeachable confession twenty minutes later — the kind that survives every appeal.

choices.text: Write it up

interrogation_outcome_release.text: You let him walk. It's the right call on the evidence you actually have — and it means the case is still open tonight.

choices.text: Head back to the evidence

media_pressure.text: A reporter catches you on the courthouse steps. "Any comment on the river case?" Behind her, three more are walking over.

choices.text: "No comment" and keep walking
Give her something to work with, off the record

captain_review.text: The captain wants the full rundown before it goes anywhere near a press release.

choices.text: Walk through every detail, including what went sideways
Give the clean version that makes the department look good

paperwork.text: Every case lives or dies on the paperwork eventually. Tonight's version is thick.

choices.text: File it properly

end_shift.text: The night detective picks up the case file. Whatever happens next, it isn't yours until tomorrow.

choices.text: Head home

random_moment.text: Between the lab results and the next move, the morning does what mornings do — a few small things happen, different every time.

choices.text: A uniform brings coffee for the team
A reporter calls, fishing for details
An old case file jogs a useful memory

triumphant.title: Case Closed, Clean
triumphant.blurb: It'll hold up in front of any jury, and everyone from the captain to the DA knows it. This is the case people remember your name for.

steady_hand.title: Solved, Not Spectacular
steady_hand.blurb: No headlines, no controversy. Just careful work that got the right person off the street.

ordinary_day.title: One More File
ordinary_day.blurb: A case, worked, filed, closed. Tomorrow there's another one exactly like it.

burned_out.title: Running on Fumes
burned_out.blurb: You closed it, but you can barely remember driving home. This pace has a cost, and it's coming due.

written_up.title: Internal Review
written_up.blurb: Something about how this case was handled is going in front of a review board. The case might hold. Your process is in question.
